---
title: "increase maximum screen resolution for widescreen monitor"
date: 2008-12-09
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by karatekid on 2008-12-09
i recently upgraded my monitor to  19" widescreen(1440X900).
however i was unable to set the required resolution under ubuntu(under windows it was a pretty easy task).
i tried reconfiguring xserver , but it never gave me the option of selecting any resolution.
i edited xorg.conf file but even it did not help

the contents for xorg.conf are as:
```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"fglrx"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
	        Depth        24
        	Modes      "1024x768_60.00" "1440×900_60.00"
	EndSubSection

EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection

```

i am using hardy heron.

---

### Post by mapes12 on 2008-12-09
Right click "Applications" then select "Edit Menus". On the left select "Other", then on the right "Screens and Graphics". Close.Then click Applications>Other>Screens and Graphics. Then make your selections from the GUI. If Screens and Graphics does not appear after doing the above then restart your system and it should be there.

---

### Post by LowSky on 2008-12-09
from your xorg file I don't see anything about a graphics, which seems weird

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"fglrx"
EndSection
```

I assume this is your graphics chip/card but it is not being identified correctly and giving you this headache, so are you using ATI or Nvida, or Intel, VIA, or SiS?
```

[COLOR="Red"]Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection[/COLOR]

Section "Screen"
	[COLOR="blue"]Identifier	"Default Screen"[/COLOR]
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
	        Depth        24
        	Modes      "1024x768_60.00" "1440×900_60.00"
	EndSubSection

```

plus this part in red make no sense and the line in blue should be Configured Monitor

### Post by philinux on 2008-12-09
Welcome to the world of Intrepid. Xorg is ultimately set to disappear.

My Xorg from a 3 weeks old Intrepid reinstall. Only addition is for screen rotation. This is a normal default xorg.conf. Also in Intrepid screens & graphics has gone., replaced by jockey i.e. screen res.
```
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	DefaultDepth	24
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"glx"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver	"nvidia"
	Option	"NoLogo"	"True"
        Option "RandRRotation" "true"
EndSection
```

however if i log into a failsafe gnome session everything fine and  i get a resolution of 1440X900.

``` brings it back. But I see you have your desired screen res now.
